title: phegopteris quadriaurita
intro: Phegopteris quadriaurita, also known as the four-veined polypody fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to East Asia, characterized by its four-veined rachis and quadrangular veins. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Phegopteris quadriaurita are said to include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often decocted in water to create a tea, which is consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
